verses:
madana gopāla śaraṇa terī āyo
caraṇa kamala kī sevā dījo,
cero kari rākho ghara jāyo

dhanya dhanya māta pitā suta bandhū,
dhanya jananī jina goda khilāyo
dhanya dhanya caraṇa calata tīrtha ko,
dhanya guru jina harināma sunāyo

je nara vimukha bhaye govinda so,
janma aneka mahāduḥkha pāyo
‘śrī⋅bhaṭṭa’ ke prabhu diyo abhaya⋅pada,
yama ḍarapyo jaba dāsa kahāyo
en_translation:
O Madana Gopāla! I have come to Your shelter. Please grant me service to Your lotus feet and keep me as a servant in Your home.

Blessed are the mother, father, son, and relatives of Your devotee. Blessed is the mother who nursed him in her lap. Blessed are the feet that walk to the holy places, and blessed is the guru who made one hear the Holy Name.

Those persons who became averse to Lord Govinda have suffered greatly through many births. The Lord of the poet Śrī Bhaṭṭa gave the fearless state; even the God of Death became afraid when one was called His servant.
